walkthrough.title: Lay Out the Game Board
walkthrough.text: Let's get King of New York set up. First, place the game board in the center of the table where everyone can reach it. The board shows Manhattan in the middle, divided into three zones: Lower Manhattan, Midtown, and Upper Manhattan. Around Manhattan you will see the five outer boroughs where monsters will start the game.

walkthrough.title: Place Building Tiles
walkthrough.text: Now grab the building tiles. These are double-sided tiles with a building on one side and a military unit on the other. Place three building tiles, building side up, in each of the five boroughs on the board. These buildings are what your monsters will stomp on during the game. When you destroy a building, you flip it to reveal the military unit underneath.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Power Card Market
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the deck of Power cards and place it face down near the board. Deal three Power cards face up in a row next to the deck. This is the market where players can buy abilities during the game. Place the energy cubes in a shared supply pile nearby. Energy is the currency you spend to buy these cards.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Monster
walkthrough.text: Each player picks a monster and takes the matching monster board, cardboard standee, and plastic stand. Assemble your standee by slotting it into the stand. Set your health dial to ten and your victory points dial to zero. You start the game at full health with no points.

walkthrough.title: Place Monsters in Boroughs
walkthrough.text: Each player places their monster standee in one of the outer boroughs on the board. No one starts in Manhattan. Manhattan begins empty and will be entered during the game. With fewer players, use fewer boroughs. The Statue of Liberty token, which represents the Superstar card, should be placed nearby.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Dice and Start
walkthrough.text: Place the six black dice in the center of the table. Set the two green bonus dice aside. These are only used when a Power card grants them. The most monstrous player goes first, or pick randomly. Manhattan is empty, so the first player will enter it during their first turn. Let's stomp some buildings!

bullets: Place board centrally — Manhattan in the middle, 5 boroughs around it
Manhattan has 3 zones: Lower, Midtown, Upper

bullets: 3 building tiles (building side up) per borough
Double-sided: building on front, military unit on back

summary.title: Set Up the Power Card Market
bullets: Shuffle Power card deck, deal 3 face-up as market
Place energy cubes in shared supply

summary.title: Choose Your Monster
bullets: Take monster board + standee + stand
Set health to 10, VP to 0

summary.title: Place Monsters in Boroughs
bullets: Each player places standee in an outer borough
Manhattan starts empty; Superstar (Statue of Liberty) nearby

summary.title: Prepare Dice and Start
bullets: 6 black dice in center; 2 green dice set aside
Most monstrous player goes first or pick randomly

walkthrough.title: How to Win
walkthrough.text: There are two ways to win King of New York. First, you can reach twenty victory points. Points come from being in Manhattan, destroying buildings, celebrity dice results, and Power card effects. Second, you can be the last monster standing. If every other monster gets eliminated, you win immediately, regardless of your point total.

walkthrough.title: Turn Overview
walkthrough.text: On your turn, you follow five steps in order. First, roll the dice up to three times. Second, resolve all your final dice results. Third, enter or leave Manhattan if applicable. Fourth, buy Power cards with energy. Fifth, end your turn and pass the dice. Let's look at each step in detail.

walkthrough.title: Rolling the Dice
walkthrough.text: Start by rolling all six black dice. Each die has six faces: claws for attacking, hearts for healing, energy for gaining energy cubes, destruction for stomping buildings, celebrity for fame, and ouch for taking damage from the military. After your first roll, you can set aside any dice you want to keep and reroll the rest. You get up to two rerolls for three total rolls.

walkthrough.title: Resolving Claws
walkthrough.text: Each claw you roll deals one damage to monsters in your target zone. If you are outside Manhattan, your claws hit all monsters inside Manhattan. If you are inside Manhattan, your claws hit all monsters outside Manhattan simultaneously. This makes being in Manhattan both powerful and dangerous. You hit everyone, but everyone hits you.

walkthrough.title: Resolving Hearts, Energy, and Celebrity
walkthrough.text: Each heart heals one health point, but you cannot heal while in Manhattan and you cannot exceed ten health. Each energy symbol gives you one energy cube from the supply. Celebrity results earn you one victory point each, but only if you hold the Superstar card. You claim the Superstar by rolling three or more celebrity faces in one turn. Another player can steal it by also rolling three or more.

walkthrough.title: Resolving Destruction
walkthrough.text: Each destruction result lets you stomp one building in your current borough. Flip the building tile over to reveal the military unit on the back. Destroying buildings can reward you with victory points, energy, or health depending on the building. But be careful, because the military units you reveal will fight back when ouch results are rolled.

walkthrough.title: Resolving Ouch
walkthrough.text: Ouch results are bad news. Each ouch deals one damage to you from the military units in your borough. Infantry deal damage on ouch results. Tanks deal damage and are harder to destroy. Jets can target monsters in any borough. You can destroy military units using destruction dice results, removing the threat. When all buildings and units in a borough are cleared, new building tiles are placed.

walkthrough.title: Manhattan Rules
walkthrough.text: If Manhattan is empty, you must enter it. When you start your turn in Manhattan, you gain victory points. Lower Manhattan gives one point, Midtown gives two points, and Upper gives two points plus one energy. Each turn you stay, you move up through the zones. But you cannot heal with hearts while in Manhattan. When you are attacked, you may choose to yield and leave Manhattan. The attacker then takes your place.

walkthrough.title: Buying Power Cards
walkthrough.text: After resolving dice, you can spend energy cubes to buy Power cards from the face-up market. Each card has a cost printed on it. Keep cards stay in front of you with ongoing effects. Discard cards are one-time effects used immediately. If you do not like the available cards, you can spend two energy to sweep the market, discarding all three and revealing three new ones. Keep buying or sweeping as long as you can afford it.

walkthrough.title: Elimination and Endgame
walkthrough.text: When your health reaches zero, you are eliminated from the game. Your monster is destroyed and you take no more turns. If multiple monsters die from the same attack, the attacker wins if they are still alive. The game ends the moment someone reaches twenty victory points or only one monster remains. There is no final round. Whoever triggers the end condition wins.

summary.title: How to Win
bullets: Reach 20 VP → win immediately
Last monster standing → win immediately
VP from Manhattan, buildings, celebrity, Power cards

walkthrough.title: Welcome to New York
walkthrough.text: Welcome to your first game of King of New York! In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted opening with four monsters. Each monster will demonstrate different strategies so you can see how the game flows. Follow along even if you have fewer players. Ready to rampage?

walkthrough.title: Starting Positions
walkthrough.text: All four monsters are in outer boroughs. Manhattan is empty. Each monster has ten health and zero victory points. The board has three building tiles in each borough. Energy cubes and the Power card market are set up. Let's roll!

walkthrough.title: Monster 1: Enter Manhattan
walkthrough.text: Monster 1 goes first. They roll the six dice and get two claws, one heart, one energy, one destruction, and one ouch. Since Manhattan is empty, Monster 1 must enter it. They place their standee in Lower Manhattan and gain one victory point for entering. But wait, their claws have no target because no one else is in Manhattan. Let's resolve the rest of their dice.

walkthrough.title: Monster 1 Resolves Dice
walkthrough.text: Monster 1 cannot use the heart to heal because they are now in Manhattan. The energy gives them one energy cube. The destruction lets them stomp a building, but Monster 1 is in Manhattan, not a borough with buildings. The ouch deals one damage from any military units in their zone. Since Manhattan has no military, the ouch does nothing. Monster 1 ends their turn with one victory point and one energy.

walkthrough.title: Monster 2: Attacking Manhattan
walkthrough.text: Monster 2 is outside Manhattan. They roll and get three claws, two energy, and one heart. The three claws deal three damage to Monster 1 in Manhattan. Monster 1's health drops from ten to seven. Monster 1 can now choose to yield and leave Manhattan, or stay and take the punishment. Monster 1 decides to stay because they want the victory points next turn.

walkthrough.title: Monster 2 Finishes Their Turn
walkthrough.text: Monster 2 heals one health with the heart, bringing them back to full. They gain two energy cubes. With three energy total after checking the market, they buy a Keep Power card that gives them extra damage. Nice purchase. Monster 2 passes the dice.

walkthrough.title: Monster 3: Destroying Buildings
walkthrough.text: Monster 3 focuses on destruction. They roll three destruction results, one energy, one claw, and one ouch. The claw hits Monster 1 in Manhattan for one damage. The three destruction results let Monster 3 stomp three buildings in their borough, flipping them to reveal military units. One building gave an energy bonus and another gave a victory point. Nice rewards!

walkthrough.title: Monster 3 Faces the Military
walkthrough.text: But now the ouch result activates. Monster 3 has military units in their borough from flipped buildings. The ouch deals one damage from those units. Monster 3 drops to nine health. The military is a double-edged sword. You get rewards for destroying buildings, but the units that appear fight back. Monster 3 ends their turn with two victory points.

walkthrough.title: Monster 4: Going for Celebrity
walkthrough.text: Monster 4 has a plan. They want the Superstar card. They roll and get two celebrities, then reroll the other four dice hoping for a third celebrity. They get it! Three celebrity faces claims the Superstar card, the Statue of Liberty. Now every celebrity they roll earns one victory point. Monster 4 scores three victory points this turn.

walkthrough.title: Monster 1: Manhattan Payoff
walkthrough.text: Back to Monster 1. They start their turn in Lower Manhattan and gain two victory points for being there. They also move up to Midtown. Monster 1 is getting rewarded for staying in Manhattan, but their health is down to six from all the attacks. They need to decide whether to keep pushing for points or yield to safety.

walkthrough.title: Beginner Mistake: Staying in Manhattan Too Long
walkthrough.text: Monster 1 decides to stay in Manhattan despite having only six health. On the next round, they take four damage from combined attacks and drop to two health. Now they are one bad roll away from elimination. The lesson here is clear: do not overstay in Manhattan. The victory points are tempting, but getting eliminated means zero chance of winning. Yield when your health drops below four or five.

walkthrough.title: Beginner Mistake: Ignoring Buildings
walkthrough.text: Monster 2 has been rolling nothing but claws and energy for three turns. They have not destroyed a single building. Meanwhile, Monster 3 has been earning victory points and rewards from building destruction. Ignoring buildings means missing out on free resources. Make time to roll destruction, especially when the building rewards look valuable.

walkthrough.title: Beginner Mistake: Hoarding Energy Without Buying
walkthrough.text: Monster 4 has eight energy cubes but has not bought a single Power card. They keep rolling energy and saving it. The problem is that energy does nothing by itself. Power cards are what make energy valuable. A well-timed Keep card can change the entire game. Do not hoard energy. Buy cards early so their effects compound over multiple turns.

walkthrough.title: Recovery: Smart Manhattan Play
walkthrough.text: Monster 1 finally yields Manhattan at two health. They heal back up in the boroughs over two turns. Then they re-enter Manhattan at full health, collect points for two turns, and yield again before getting too low. This in-and-out strategy is much safer than camping in Manhattan until you die.

walkthrough.title: The Final Push
walkthrough.text: The game is heating up. Monster 3 has fifteen victory points from building destruction. Monster 4 has twelve from celebrity rolls. Monster 1 has ten from Manhattan visits. Monster 2 has eight but a powerful set of Keep cards. Everyone is eyeing twenty points. The final turns will come down to who can score fastest while surviving.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: Great work! You have seen all the core mechanics of King of New York. You know how to roll and resolve dice, enter and leave Manhattan, destroy buildings, deal with the military, buy Power cards, and claim the Superstar. You also learned three common mistakes: staying in Manhattan too long, ignoring buildings, and hoarding energy without buying cards.

walkthrough.title: Before You Rampage
walkthrough.text:
Before you start your first full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to dice faces, Power card types, and game terms.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q and A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

summary.title: Before You Rampage
bullets: Check out General Tips → strategy basics for beginners
Reference the Appendix → quick-reference for dice and terms
Ask your personal GM Guide → tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Here are the biggest traps new players fall into. First, staying in Manhattan at low health and getting eliminated. Second, ignoring buildings entirely and missing free rewards. Third, hoarding energy without buying Power cards. Fourth, healing when you are already at full health and wasting dice. Fifth, forgetting that you cannot heal in Manhattan. Keep these in mind and you will survive longer than most beginners.

walkthrough.title: Manhattan Risk Management
walkthrough.text: Manhattan is the fastest way to earn victory points, but it comes with serious risk. You cannot heal with hearts while inside, and every monster outside is attacking you. The smart approach is to enter Manhattan when you have high health, ideally eight or more, collect points for one or two turns, then yield when your health drops below five. Do not get greedy. A eliminated monster scores zero points.

walkthrough.title: Energy Equals Power
walkthrough.text: Energy cubes are your ticket to Power cards, and Power cards are what separate winners from losers. In the early game, prioritize rolling energy so you can buy strong Keep cards quickly. A good Keep card bought on turn two generates value for the rest of the game. A great card bought on the last turn barely matters. Treat energy as an investment, not a stockpile.

walkthrough.title: Building Destruction Strategy
walkthrough.text: Destroying buildings is underrated by new players. Each building you stomp can give you victory points, energy, or even health. The military units that appear are dangerous, but you can destroy them too with future destruction rolls. Focus on clearing buildings in your borough early to collect rewards. Once the military is cleared, new buildings are placed and you can farm them again.

walkthrough.title: Dice Rolling Strategy
walkthrough.text: Do not reroll randomly. Before each reroll, decide what you need most this turn. If you are low on health and outside Manhattan, keep hearts. If you are trying to buy a card, keep energy. If someone in Manhattan is low on health, keep claws for the kill shot. Lock your dice toward one objective each turn instead of keeping one of everything. Focus gets better results than diversification.

walkthrough.title: Watch Elimination Thresholds
walkthrough.text: When another monster drops below four health, they are in the danger zone. One good claw roll can eliminate them. If you have claws locked in, consider targeting the weak monster. Eliminating a player permanently removes a threat and brings the game closer to a last-monster-standing win. On the flip side, if you are below four health, play defensive. Heal, yield Manhattan, and avoid rolling ouch dice near military units.

walkthrough.title: The Superstar Gambit
walkthrough.text: The Superstar card is powerful but unreliable. You need three celebrity faces in one roll to claim it, which requires luck. If you naturally roll two celebrities, it is worth rerolling for the third. But do not waste all three rolls chasing celebrities if the dice are not cooperating. The Superstar is a bonus, not a strategy. Let it come to you rather than forcing it.

walkthrough.title: Power Card Market Awareness
walkthrough.text: Always check the Power card market before rolling your dice. If there is a game-changing card available that costs five energy, and you have three, you know to prioritize energy this turn. If the market has nothing useful, save your energy or spend two to sweep and hope for better cards. The best players adjust their dice strategy based on what is available in the market.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't stay in Manhattan at low health — elimination = 0 points
Don't ignore buildings — free VP, energy, and health
Don't hoard energy — buy Power cards early
Don't waste hearts at full health; can't heal in Manhattan

Focus beats diversification

walkthrough.title: Aggro Manhattan Rush
walkthrough.text: The aggro strategy aims to win through rapid victory point accumulation by dominating Manhattan. Enter Manhattan on your first turn with full health and stay as long as possible. Buy defensive Power cards like armor or extra health to extend your stay. The key is pushing past fifteen victory points before other players can catch up. Yield only when elimination is imminent, heal up quickly, and re-enter. This strategy works best in games with fewer players where you take less damage per round.

walkthrough.title: Building Farmer Strategy
walkthrough.text: The building farmer avoids Manhattan entirely and focuses on destroying buildings for steady rewards. Prioritize destruction dice every roll. Each building gives resources and the military units that appear can be destroyed for additional benefits. This strategy generates a slow but consistent stream of victory points, energy, and health. Use the energy to buy powerful cards that accelerate your scoring. This is the safest strategy because you avoid the crossfire in Manhattan.

walkthrough.title: Energy Engine and Card Combos
walkthrough.text: Some Power cards create devastating combinations. The energy engine strategy prioritizes energy dice early to buy multiple Keep cards that synergize together. Look for cards that give extra dice, extra damage, or bonus points for specific actions. A well-built card engine can generate three or four victory points per turn without ever entering Manhattan. The downside is a slow start. You spend the first few turns investing and start scoring later.

walkthrough.title: Elimination Hunter
walkthrough.text: Instead of racing to twenty points, the elimination hunter focuses on knocking out other players. Target the monster with the lowest health every turn. Keep claws and reroll everything else toward more claws. Eliminating players is permanent and reduces the number of threats. In a four-player game, eliminating two players means you only need to outlast one opponent. This strategy works best with Power cards that increase your damage output.

walkthrough.title: Opportunistic Yielder
walkthrough.text: The opportunistic yielder enters Manhattan when it is safe, collects a burst of victory points, and yields at the first sign of danger. The key is timing your entries when other players are focused on each other or recovering from low health. Enter at full health, stay for one or two scoring phases, then bail. Over the course of the game, multiple short Manhattan visits add up to significant points without the risk of camping.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Table
walkthrough.text: Advanced players constantly assess the table state. Who is closest to twenty points? Who is low on health? Who has dangerous Power cards? Adjust your strategy based on what everyone else is doing. If someone is at eighteen points, focus claws on them to slow them down or eliminate them. If two players are fighting each other in Manhattan, farm buildings and buy cards while they weaken each other. The best players win by adapting, not by sticking rigidly to one plan.

walkthrough.title: Military Management
walkthrough.text: The military is often treated as a nuisance, but advanced players use it strategically. If your borough is full of military units, you take ouch damage every time you roll ouch. But if you clear the military, the borough resets with fresh buildings to destroy for more rewards. Time your destruction rolls to clear out military before the ouch damage becomes unsustainable. Some players deliberately move to boroughs with fewer military units to reduce risk.

walkthrough.title: Endgame Timing
walkthrough.text: The final few turns are where games are won or lost. When multiple players are near twenty victory points, the endgame becomes a sprint. Count exactly how many points you need and what dice results get you there. If you need three points, entering Manhattan and rolling one celebrity with the Superstar might be enough. If an opponent is about to win, you might need to pivot entirely to claws and try to eliminate them. Always know your win condition and your opponent's win condition heading into the final stretch.

summary.title: Aggro Manhattan Rush
bullets: Enter Manhattan early, stay as long as possible for VP
Buy defensive cards (armor, extra health) to extend stay
Best in low-player-count games with less incoming damage

summary.title: Building Farmer Strategy
bullets: Avoid Manhattan, focus on destruction dice for building rewards
Steady VP, energy, and health from stomping
Safest strategy — avoids Manhattan crossfire

summary.title: Energy Engine and Card Combos
bullets: Prioritize energy early → buy multiple synergistic Keep cards
Card engine can generate 3-4 VP/turn without Manhattan
Slow start, strong finish

summary.title: Elimination Hunter
bullets: Target lowest-health monster every turn with claws
Eliminating players reduces threats permanently
Pair with damage-boosting Power cards

summary.title: Opportunistic Yielder
bullets: Short Manhattan visits: enter at full HP, yield at first danger
Time entries when others are distracted or weakened
Multiple short visits = significant VP without camping risk

summary.title: Reading the Table

Move to boroughs with fewer military for reduced risk

summary.title: Endgame Timing

Pivot to claws if opponent is about to win
Always know your win condition and opponent's heading into final stretch

entries.definition: A die face representing an attack. Each claw rolled deals one damage to monsters in the opposing zone. Monsters in Manhattan hit all outside; monsters outside hit all in Manhattan.
A die face representing healing. Each heart heals one health point. Cannot be used while in Manhattan. Health cannot exceed ten.
A die face representing resource collection. Each energy symbol grants one energy cube from the supply. Energy is spent to buy Power cards.
A die face representing building stomping. Each destruction result lets you destroy one building tile in your current borough, flipping it to reveal a military unit.
A die face representing fame. Celebrity results earn one victory point each, but only if you hold the Superstar card. Roll three or more celebrities in one turn to claim the Superstar.
A die face representing military retaliation. Each ouch result deals one damage to you from military units in your borough. More military units means more danger from ouch results.

The central area of the board divided into three zones: Lower, Midtown, and Upper. Monsters in Manhattan gain VP at the start of their turn but cannot heal with hearts and are targeted by all monsters outside.
The first zone of Manhattan. Grants one victory point when entering and two victory points at the start of your turn.
The second zone of Manhattan. Grants two victory points at the start of your turn. Move here from Lower Manhattan on your next turn in Manhattan.
The third and highest zone of Manhattan. Grants two victory points and one energy cube at the start of your turn.
One of five outer areas on the board where monsters can be placed. Each borough has building tiles and potentially military units.
Double-sided tiles placed in boroughs. Building side up at start. When destroyed, flip to reveal military unit. Destroying buildings grants rewards like VP, energy, or health.
The reverse side of building tiles. Activated by ouch dice results. Three types: infantry, tanks, and jets. Each deals damage differently. Can be destroyed with destruction dice.
A military unit type. Deals one damage per ouch result rolled. The most basic military threat.
A military unit type. Deals one damage per ouch result and is harder to destroy than infantry.
A military unit type. Deals one damage per ouch result and can target monsters in any borough, not just its own.
Cards purchased with energy cubes from the face-up market. Come in two types: Keep cards with ongoing effects, and Discard cards with one-time effects.
A type of Power card that stays face up in front of you with ongoing effects until the end of the game or until removed by another effect.
A type of Power card that resolves its effect immediately when purchased, then is discarded.
A special card claimed by rolling three or more celebrity faces in a single turn. While held, each celebrity rolled earns one VP. Can be stolen by another player rolling three or more celebrities.
When attacked while in Manhattan, you may choose to leave Manhattan and return to a borough. The attacking player then takes your place in Manhattan.
When a monster's health reaches zero, they are permanently removed from the game. Discard all their Keep cards and energy cubes.
Spend two energy cubes to discard all three face-up Power cards in the market and reveal three new ones. Can be done multiple times if you have the energy.
Two extra dice with enhanced faces. Only available when a Power card grants access to them. Rolled in addition to the six black dice.
Tracked on your monster board dial. Earned from Manhattan, buildings, celebrity rolls, and Power cards. First to twenty wins.
